title: Bridge Essay: Individual variability in neural representations of mind-wandering.
concept: Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, default mode network, creative cognition, involuntary thought.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, default mode network, creative cognition, involuntary thought.

**Individual variability in neural representations of mind-wandering.** - bioRxiv (2024). DOI: 10.1101/2024.01.20.576471. PMID: 38328109. Mind-wandering is a frequent, daily mental activity, experienced in unique ways in each person. Yet neuroimaging evidence relating mind-wandering to brain activity, for example in the default mode network (DMN), has relied on population-rather than individual-based inferences due to limited within-individual sampling. Here, three densely-sampled individuals each reported hundreds of mind-wandering episodes while undergoing multi-session functional magnetic resonance imaging. We found reliable as
